title: Religious beleifs in America by party

transcript.text: Okay, so this video is comparing religious beliefs in America by political party according to the Harris Poll
Surveying 2,201 adults between January 2nd and 27th or something like that of the year 2003.
So, I know most people say that Republicans are more religious than the Democrats, so let's see if that's true.
So we're going to compare the Republicans, Democrats, and Independents.
So I guess people who are neither, or all the other political parties, and then all the adults.
So God, all of adults, it's 90%, but it's 93 Republican and 90 Democrats.
So yes, Republicans do believe in God more.
And it's 87% Independents, so I guess the Atheists are more independent.
Okay, so survival, 84% of Americans.
Republicans are higher than Democrats, 87 to 83.
And then Independents, it's the same as the Democrats, with 83%.
Miracles, all of Americans, 84% of Americans believe in miracles.
The Republicans believe in miracles more, 87 to 82.
And then the Independents are the same, 82.
Heaven, all of Americans, 89%, 82%.
Republicans believe in miracles in heaven more, 89 to 80%.
And then the Independents believe it the less, at 78%.
The Resurrection, all adults, 80%.
Republicans believe it a lot more, actually, 87 to 78.
And then the Independents, the least, is at 75.
The Virgin Birth, all of adults, 77%.
Republicans believe in it the most, by the biggest margin.
86 to 73, however, the Democrats and the people who believe it the least are the Independents, with 72%.
Hell, 69% of Americans.
Republicans believe in hell a whole lot more than the Democrats, 82 to 63.
So, yeah, maybe that means there's lots of undies in the Republican Party.
But the Independents believe in more of hell than the Democrats, at 65% to 63.
So that's the one thing that the Independents so far believe more in.
Now, the Devil, 68% of Americans believe in the Devil.
Republicans believe it in more, 79 to 62% over the Democrats.
But the Independents believe it 65% over 62 to the Democrats.
So the Independents believe in the Devil more, also, along with hell.
Ghosts, 51% of Americans believe in ghosts.
Oh, look, Democrats believe in ghosts more than the Republicans, 54 over 52.
It's the one thing that Democrats believe in more than the Republicans.
And then the people who believe in ghosts the most are the Independents, at 56%.
Astrology, 31% of Americans believe in astrology.
The people who believe it the least by, it looks like, the biggest margin, or one of the biggest margins,
Is only 19% of Republicans.
And a whopping 40% of Democrats believe in astrology.
And then the people who believe it kind of in between are the Independents, with 29%.
Reincarnation, 27% of Americans believe in reincarnation.
The people who believe it the least are the Republicans, at 18%.
And the Democrats believe it the most, at 31%.
Followed closely by the Independents, at 30%.
So how do I interpret this?
Well, religious beliefs about, you know, reality and God and everything, the afterlife.
Republicans and Democrats.
Republicans are more religious.
They believe in more traditional religious ideas than Democrats.
Because they believe in God, because they believe in God, soul survival, miracles, heaven, the resurrection, the virgin birth, hell, the devil, more than the Democrats.
But the Democrats believe in ghost astrology and reincarnation more.
So I would say that Republicans are more Christian, traditional Christian, and Democrats tend to be more alternative religions, like Buddhism and others kinds of things.
Now, as far as Independents, people who just don't want to be part of either of those two parties, maybe, I don't know, politically more independent thinking, I would say.
They believe in traditional religious concepts, the least out of all of them, God, soul, survival, actually the same as Democrats, but they believe in God less.
Miracles, they believe the same as Republicans or Democrats, but they believe less in heaven, less in the resurrection, less in the virgin birth.
They believe in hell and the devil more, though, and in ghosts.
They believe in hell, the devil, and ghosts more.
So, how do I interpret that?
Maybe, I don't know, maybe they're just more cynical also, you know, they become independent because they just have lost faith in the government.
Because they also believe in astrology and less, the least out of everybody.
So, maybe atheists would be the independents, and they believe in reincarnation less than the Democrats, but more than the Republicans.
So, how would I plug that into traditional financial?
Well, I know Republicans tend to be more control-oriented, try to control you, more into pro-trying to ban abortion and this kind of thing.

But, I guess that's because they're more traditionally religious and the Christians are more into legalizing abortion because there's lots of fundamentalist Christians who think that if you kill a baby, you're sending that baby's soul to hell for eternity.
Um, but fiscally, Republicans are more conservative, and Democrats are more kind of communist.
Um, so the commies, uh-huh, believe in ghosts and astrology and reincarnation more.
Um, maybe because they have, uh, more of a, uh, vision for a kind of a universal healthcare, I don't know.
Whereas, uh, nuts and bolts people are more, uh, traditional, um, I don't know.
Interpret that how you want.
